description: One-pass review of crate secretx-doppler (/home/mark/PROJECT/crate-secretsx/secretx-doppler). Filed by /review-rusty-crate.
notes:
Maintainer risk: HIGH — the put() write path is broken against the real Doppler API (#1) and the WritableBackendRegistration registers it for URI dispatch anyway (#2). Combined, the first user who tries to write through this crate hits a 4xx with a generic error message and has no path to a useful diagnostic. The 'Not yet integration-tested' module-doc note is the trail of breadcrumbs but does not prevent the bug from shipping.
Correctness confidence: 90% chance no further bug remains in get/put logic. Primary uncertainty: I have not exercised the code against a live Doppler endpoint; the JSON response shape ('secret.computed' string) was inferred from the secretx-doppler module-doc and the get URL is verified from Doppler's own API reference, but the response-extraction code has never been observed to succeed in this repo. Least confident flags: #6 (unknown-query-param footgun is a workspace-wide pattern question, not just a doppler bug). Uncertainty driven by: no live integration test exists and no recorded HTTP fixtures are checked in. Would raise confidence if: a single recorded VCR-style fixture (or a test using wiremock) demonstrated end-to-end get/put against a mock Doppler API.
Idiom rating: ACCEPTABLE — the code is recognizably idiomatic Rust at the small-detail level (Result+? everywhere, async_trait usage, Zeroizing for the token). The shape-level idiom issues are: (a) call-site duplication of status-mapping logic that map_http_status should own (#14), (b) manual JSON walking instead of a serde-derived response type (#15), (c) collect-then-index pattern in URI parse (#13). None are sound-Rust violations; all are quality-of-life. Idiom rating would move to CLEAN if #14 and #15 land.
Rust idiom confidence: 85% chance of missed issue. Least confident flags: #16 and #17 are taste-tier and labeled 'opinion'; if those don't land it's not a regression. Uncertainty driven by: I did not run cargo doc --cfg docsrs (it would require a nightly toolchain) and did not run cargo hack feature-powerset, both of which sometimes surface idiom issues invisible to a normal cargo clippy. cargo clippy --all-features --tests was clean.
title: Review: secretx-doppler rusty pass
